title: Clubhouse Bed (Twin)
summary: A twin bed built like a tiny gabled clubhouse — individual angle-cut 1x6 board walls on a 2x4 frame with 30° peak cuts, distinct from this catalog's flat-panel playhouse loft bed.
description:
Unlike this catalog's playhouse loft bed, which builds its walls from single flat plywood panels trimmed with lattice, this clubhouse bed builds each wall from individual 1x6 boards cut to length and nailed up side by side over a 2x4 frame — a board-and-batten look with real board lines, not a painted panel. The two gable-end walls need each board cut to a different length to follow the 30° roof peak, which is the one step in this plan worth double-checking board by board before you nail anything, since a single wrong length breaks the peak's straight roofline.

Build the four wall frames from 2x4s first, flat on the ground, then skin each frame with its 1x6 boards before standing anything up — exactly like framing and sheathing a real wall, at dollhouse-for-kids scale. Standing four skinned wall frames up and screwing them together at the corners is far more manageable than trying to build the walls in place.

The roof is simple shed-style 1x6 decking over 2x4 rafters, left open on the underside (no ceiling) so the bed structure stays low-weight and low-cost. **This is a bed frame with walls, not a full enclosure** — there's no floor, door, or latch, and the mattress sits on a standard slat deck inside the frame like any platform bed. Anchor the whole structure to the wall studs behind it if it will see active climbing or leaning, the same way this catalog's bunk beds are anchored.

cutList.note: Center post to the peak height.
Each board cut individually to follow the 30° peak — mark and cut in place against the framed gable, do not pre-cut from a single length.

steps.title: Build the four wall frames
steps.body: Build the two side wall frames and two gable wall frames from 2x4s flat on the ground, including the gable peak posts. Check each frame square before moving on.

steps.title: Skin the side walls
steps.body: Nail the side wall boards to their frames with 2" brad nails and glue, spacing evenly.

steps.title: Skin the gable walls
steps.body: Working against the framed gable, mark and cut each gable board individually to follow the 30° peak line before nailing in place. Do this one board at a time rather than pre-cutting the whole set.

steps.title: Stand and join the walls
steps.body: Stand the four skinned wall frames up and screw together at the corners with 2-1/2" exterior screws.

steps.title: Add the roof
steps.body: Attach the rafters to the gable peak posts, then nail the roof decking boards across the rafters.

steps.title: Build the bed frame and add slats
steps.body: Assemble the bed frame rails and headers inside the wall structure with 3" exterior screws, then attach the mattress slats across the frame. Anchor the structure to wall studs behind it with 4" lag screws before use. Sand and finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Twin bed built with gabled clubhouse walls
